title: The Liar's Counter
description: Build a counter where clicking +1 three times fast only increments once visually until a re-render forces it. Explain *why* batching does this.

title: Key Crime Scene
description: Render a list using array index as `key`, then reorder/filter it and watch state attached to list items scramble. Fix it. Explain the reconciliation bug in one sentence.

title: The Stale Closure Trap
description: Write a `setInterval`-based counter inside `useEffect` that "freezes" at 1 due to a stale closure. Then fix it two different ways (functional update vs. ref).

title: Strict Mode Double-Fire
description: Build a component that calls an API in `useEffect` and visibly logs the double-invoke in dev Strict Mode. Explain why it's safe in prod.

title: useLayoutEffect or Bust
description: Build a tooltip that flickers/jumps with `useEffect` measuring DOM position, then fix the flicker by switching to `useLayoutEffect`.

title: Controlled Chaos
description: Build the same text input as both controlled and uncontrolled, then deliberately break the controlled one by forgetting `onChange`. Narrate the React warning from memory.

title: Fragment Detective
description: Render a list of siblings without a wrapper `div` using `<>...</>`, then explain when you'd be forced to use `React.Fragment` with a `key` instead.

title: Portal to the Underworld
description: Build a modal using `createPortal` that escapes a `overflow: hidden` parent. Explain why a regular nested div couldn't.

title: Event Pooling Ghost Story
description: Log a synthetic event object asynchronously (after a `setTimeout`) and explain what you see and why (React 17+ vs older behavior).

title: Render Count Detective
description: Add a render counter (via `useRef`) to 3 nested components and figure out, by reasoning alone before testing, which ones re-render when a grandparent's unrelated state changes.

title: `useDebounce`
description: From scratch, no lodash. Use it on a search input that hits a fake API.

title: `useThrottle`
description: From scratch. Use it on a scroll handler.

title: `usePrevious`
description: Track and display the previous value of a piece of state.

title: `useToggle`
description: Tiny, but explain why you'd extract this instead of inlining `!state`.

title: `useFetch` with Abort
description: Custom data-fetching hook with loading/error/data states AND `AbortController` cleanup on unmount.

title: `useLocalStorage` Sync Hook
description: Persist state to localStorage, sync across multiple components reading the same key.

title: `useOnClickOutside`
description: Classic dropdown-closer. Build it with refs + event listeners, clean up properly.

title: `useInterval` the Dan Abramov Way
description: Implement the ref-based pattern that solves the stale closure problem from Drill #3, generically.

title: `useMediaQuery`
description: Responsive hook reacting to window resize, no library.

title: `useUndo`
description: A hook that gives you `state, setState, undo, redo` with a history stack. (This is secretly a mini reducer pattern — notice that.)

title: Memo Myth-Buster
description: Build a component wrapped in `React.memo` that *still* re-renders every time because you pass an inline arrow function as a prop. Fix it with `useCallback`.

title: The Expensive Calculation
description: Build a component with a deliberately slow function (nested loop), wire it with and without `useMemo`, and visibly show the lag difference.

title: Object Identity Trap
description: Pass `{ a: 1 }` inline as a prop every render, prove via console logs that it breaks memoization, fix with `useMemo`.

title: Context Re-render Bomb
description: Build a Context Provider wrapping 5 consumers; change one piece of context state and prove ALL consumers re-render. Then split context to fix it.

title: Lifting State Properly
description: Take two sibling components silently duplicating the same state and lift it to the right common ancestor — no Context, no library.

title: Derived State Anti-Pattern
description: Build a component that wrongly copies a prop into state via `useState(prop)`, show the bug when prop changes, then fix it by deriving instead of storing.

title: Reducer Refactor
description: Take a component with 5 separate `useState` calls that always update together, refactor into one `useReducer`.

title: The `key` Reset Trick
description: Use a changing `key` prop to intentionally force-remount a component and reset all its internal state, on purpose, as a pattern (not a bug this time).

title: Batched vs Unbatched
description: Trigger 2 state updates inside a native DOM event listener (not a React handler) and observe they're NOT batched pre-React 18. Explain `flushSync`.

title: Diffing Two Lists
description: Without React, by hand, write the pseudocode/logic for how React's reconciler decides whether to update vs. replace a list item given old/new arrays + keys.

title: Controlled Form Survival Kit
description: Multi-field form (text, checkbox, select, radio) all controlled, single `handleChange` using `name` attribute.

title: Debounced Live Validation
description: Email field that validates 400ms after the user stops typing, shows inline error.

title: File Upload Preview
description: Controlled file input showing an image preview before upload, using `FileReader` or object URLs.

title: Multi-Step Wizard
description: 3-step form with state preserved across steps and a progress indicator, no router.

title: Optimistic Form Submit
description: Submit a form, immediately show success UI, then roll back gracefully if the (fake, delayed) API call fails.

title: Keyboard Navigable List
description: Arrow-key up/down navigation through a list of items, Enter to "select."

title: Drag to Reorder
description: Basic drag-and-drop reordering of a list using native HTML5 drag events (no library).

title: Custom Checkbox Group with "Select All"
description: Indeterminate checkbox state included.

title: Debounced Autosave
description: A textarea that "saves" to a fake backend 1s after typing stops, shows a "Saving… / Saved" status, cancels in-flight saves correctly.

title: Accessible Modal Focus Trap
description: Modal that traps Tab focus inside it and returns focus to the trigger button on close.

title: Compound Components
description: Build `<Tabs><Tab/><Tab/></Tabs>` using Context internally, no prop drilling.

title: Render Props Pattern
description: Build a `<MouseTracker render={(pos) => ...} />` component, then explain why hooks mostly replaced this pattern.

title: Higher-Order Component
description: Write a `withLoading(Component)` HOC, then rewrite the same logic as a hook and compare.

title: Polymorphic Component (`as` prop)
description: A `<Button as="a">` that renders different elements based on a prop, with correct TypeScript typing.

title: Slot Pattern
description: Build a `<Card>` component accepting `header`, `footer`, and `children` as distinct named slots.

title: Controlled/Uncontrolled Hybrid
description: Build one component that works BOTH controlled (`value`+`onChange` passed) and uncontrolled (internal state) depending on what's passed in.

title: Skeleton Loader Component
description: Generic `<Skeleton width height />` reusable across different shapes.

title: Error Boundary From Scratch
description: Class component implementing `componentDidCatch`, wrapping a component that deliberately throws, with a fallback UI.

title: Provider Pattern for Theming
description: Light/dark theme via Context + a `useTheme()` hook, no external lib.

title: Headless Component
description: Build a `useAccordion()` hook that returns state + handlers only, with ZERO markup, then render two completely different-looking accordions using it.

title: Virtualized List, By Hand
description: Render only the visible rows of a 10,000-item list based on scroll position (the windowing math, no `react-window`).

title: Code-Splitting with Suspense
description: `React.lazy()` + `<Suspense fallback>` for a route, visibly show the loading boundary.

title: Image Lazy Load with IntersectionObserver
description: No library, native observer.

title: Concurrent Rendering Demo
description: Use `useTransition` to keep a search input responsive while filtering a huge list, show the difference with/without it.

title: Avoiding Prop Drilling Without Context
description: Refactor a 4-level-deep prop chain using component composition instead of Context (the "right" first fix before reaching for Context).

title: `useDeferredValue` Race
description: Build a UI where stale results visibly persist briefly during fast typing, intentionally, to understand the trade-off.

title: Bundle Diet
description: Take a component importing a whole library for one function, replace with a native equivalent, and explain bundle size impact.

title: Memoized Selector Pattern
description: Mimic Redux/Reselect-style derived-state memoization manually with `useMemo`.

title: Profiler API Hunt
description: Use React's `<Profiler>` component to actually measure and log render durations of a suspect component.

title: Animation Without Re-render
description: Animate something (e.g. a progress bar) using refs + direct DOM manipulation instead of state, and explain why that avoids re-renders.

title: The Classic Race Condition
description: Fire two fetches for different search terms in quick succession, show the slow one overwriting the fast one's correct result, then fix it.

title: Cancel-on-Unmount
description: A fetch inside `useEffect` that updates state after the component has already unmounted — show the warning, then fix with cleanup/AbortController.

title: Retry with Backoff
description: A fetch wrapper that retries a failing fake endpoint 3 times with increasing delay.

title: Polling Hook
description: `usePolling(fn, interval)` that stops cleanly on unmount or on a `stop()` call.

title: Cache-Then-Network
description: Show cached data immediately, then silently refetch and update if new data differs (basic SWR-style behavior, hand-rolled).

title: Parallel vs Sequential Fetches
description: Fetch 3 independent resources in parallel with `Promise.all`, then show why sequential `await` would be slower, with timing logs.

title: Dependent Fetch Chain
description: Fetch a user, then use their ID to fetch their posts — model the loading states correctly for each stage.

title: Optimistic List Add/Remove
description: Add an item to a list immediately, roll back if the fake API call fails.

title: Deduping In-Flight Requests
description: Two components both request the same resource simultaneously; ensure only ONE network call fires (hand-rolled, no React Query).

title: Error Boundary + Async Errors
description: Show why Error Boundaries do NOT catch errors inside event handlers or async callbacks, and demonstrate the correct way to handle those.

title: Typewriter From a String
description: Fake "streaming" by revealing a hardcoded string one character at a time with `setInterval`. The "hello world" of agent UIs.

title: Real SSE Consumer
description: Connect to a real Server-Sent Events endpoint (or a tiny Express server you spin up) and render incoming chunks live.

title: Cancelable Stream
description: Add a "Stop generating" button that actually aborts an in-progress fetch stream (`AbortController` + `ReadableStream`).

title: Concurrent Message Streams
description: Two streaming "agent messages" arriving interleaved; make sure chunks never merge into the wrong message bubble.

title: Batched Token Renderer
description: Render incoming stream tokens with a 50ms batching window instead of per-token `setState`, and measure re-render count before/after.

title: Restart Mid-Stream
description: User sends a new message while a previous stream is still running — old stream must be discarded/ignored cleanly, not race into the new message.

title: Streaming Markdown Renderer
description: Render streamed text as Markdown that updates live without the whole block flickering/remounting each chunk.

title: Tool Call Visualizer
description: Mock an agent response containing `tool_call_start` → `tool_call_args` (streamed in pieces) → `tool_call_result` events; render a live-updating "card" for the tool call.

title: Loading State Machine
description: Model an agent's lifecycle (`idle → thinking → streaming → tool_call → done/error`) with `useReducer`, not a pile of booleans.

title: Mock AG-UI Event Bus
description: Build a tiny typed event emitter that simulates AG-UI's `TEXT_MESSAGE_START/CONTENT/END` and `TOOL_CALL_*` events on a timer, and a component that subscribes and renders accordingly.

title: Hand-Rolled AG-UI Client
description: Implement a minimal client that parses a stream of typed JSON events (`text_message_content`, `tool_call_start`, etc.) into rendered UI, no library.

title: State Snapshot vs Delta
description: Implement both a full `STATE_SNAPSHOT` replace and a `STATE_DELTA` patch-merge for agent state, and show why deltas are more efficient at scale.

title: Custom Tool Renderer Registry
description: Build a `registerToolRenderer('search_flights', Component)` system so different tool calls render different custom UI — the "Custom Tool Renderers" post, made real.

title: Trusted Proxy Pattern (BFF)
description: Stub out (architecturally, even just in comments/structure) why your React app should never call the agent runtime directly, and build the thin Node proxy layer that should sit between them.

title: Token Cost Meter
description: Given a stream of tokens with a per-token cost constant, build a live-updating "$ spent so far" counter — the seed of your "Cost-Aware Frontend Architecture" post.

title: Speculative UI / Pre-fill from Streamed Args
description: As tool call *arguments* stream in (before the call completes), progressively pre-fill a form/UI with partial data.

title: Reconnect on Drop
description: Simulate a dropped SSE/WebSocket connection mid-stream and implement reconnect-and-resume logic (even if "resume" just means restart gracefully with a clear UI state).

title: Guardrail UI Pattern
description: Build a UI affordance that intercepts a tool call requiring "confirmation" (e.g. `delete_record`) and blocks execution until the user approves — human-in-the-loop, rendered.

title: Multi-Agent Tabbed View
description: Render two independent simulated agents streaming simultaneously into separate panels without their state bleeding into each other.

title: Execution Trace Exporter
description: Capture every event your mock agent emits into an array, then export it as JSON — this IS your "Agent State Inspector" portfolio project's core engine.

title: Build Your Own `useState`
description: Implement a fake `useState` using a module-level array + index pointer (the classic "build your own React hooks" exercise). Mind-bending, very high signal.

title: Build Your Own `useReducer`
description: Same exercise, layered on top of #91.

title: Mini Pub-Sub Store (Zustand-lite)
description: `createStore(initialState)` returning `getState`, `setState`, `subscribe` — then a `useStore` hook wrapping it.

title: Mini Redux (Reducer + Dispatch + Context)
description: Combine reducer + Context + a `connect`-style HOC, by hand, to actually feel why Redux exists.

title: Mini React Query (Cache + Stale Time)
description: A `useQuery(key, fetchFn)` hook with in-memory cache and a `staleTime` before refetching.

title: Mini Router
description: `useState` + `window.history.pushState` based router supporting 3 routes, no library.

title: Event Emitter From Scratch
description: `on`, `off`, `emit` — then realize this is the backbone of drill #80's mock AG-UI bus.

title: Mini Signals (Fine-Grained Reactivity)
description: Implement a tiny signal/effect system (`createSignal`, `createEffect`) outside React, then explain how this relates to React Fiber's internals and libraries like `bippy`.

title: Dependency Graph Resolver
description: Given a list of `{id, dependsOn: []}` tasks, write a topological sort to determine valid execution order — directly reusable for any future DAG/orchestration work.

title: Hanachi's First Breath
description: Combine drills #79 (state machine), #90 (trace exporter), and #43 (HOC/hook pattern) into the actual MVP of Hanachi: a component that visibly reacts (idle/thinking/streaming/error) to a mock agent's live event stream. This is where every drill above stops being an exercise and becomes your portfolio.
